participants: Matt Wisniewski — Sales Leader / Decision Maker, USALab

jtbd.job: Manage the full lead-to-quote lifecycle in one system as the company builds its first real sales team
jtbd.desired_outcome: Sales reps can action leads, create quotes, track deal stages, and close without toggling between disconnected systems
jtbd.current_approach: Inbound requests handled ad hoc through help desk; quotes created manually in Brightpearl/NetSuite; no structured sales process

jtbd.job: Send accurate quotes with correct tax, bill-to, and ship-to from HubSpot
jtbd.desired_outcome: Quotes automatically calculate tax based on shipping address and display separate bill-to and ship-to fields
jtbd.current_approach: Tax calculated inside Brightpearl/NetSuite; HubSpot native quoting could not handle separate bill-to/ship-to or calculate tax properly

jtbd.job: Keep deal status visible and up to date without manual effort from reps
jtbd.desired_outcome: Deal stages, quotes, and payment status are automatically tracked and linked so nothing falls through the cracks
jtbd.current_approach: Quotes live in NetSuite with no automatic link back to HubSpot deals; reps forget to update deal stages or attach quotes

jtbd.job: Support wholesaler and academic discount pricing without relying on rep memory
jtbd.desired_outcome: Price books automatically apply the correct discount tier based on customer type
jtbd.current_approach: Discounts tracked via informal tags; reps manually apply percentage discounts; not managed in ERP

pains.pain: HubSpot native quoting lacks bill-to and ship-to address support
pains.trigger: Customers almost always bill and ship to different locations; tax must be calculated on ship-to address
pains.cost: Cannot use HubSpot quoting at all for their business; forced to quote outside HubSpot
pains.verbatim: you couldn't add a bill to and ship to... that's a huge thing for us

pains.pain: Tax calculation does not work properly in HubSpot native quoting
pains.trigger: Company collects tax in 22 states; tax must apply per line item and include shipping
pains.cost: Blocked adoption of HubSpot quoting; had to keep tax calculation in Brightpearl/NetSuite
pains.verbatim: it would not calculate tax... it like wouldn't... it was like a weird issue

pains.pain: Disconnect between HubSpot deals and NetSuite quotes creates visibility gaps
pains.trigger: Building a new sales team and need deal tracking; quotes live in NetSuite but deals live in HubSpot
pains.cost: Reps forget to attach quotes or update deal stages; no single view of deal status; complex deals with future timelines get lost
pains.verbatim: there's a disconnect between HubSpot and NetSuite... to have a quote in NetSuite is cool, but then I don't wanna have to make the reps always attach it or forget about it

pains.pain: No structured sales process to manage leads and deals as company scales from inbound-only to outbound
pains.trigger: Company transitioning from reactive inbound order-taking to proactive sales with a 3-person team
pains.cost: Cannot action leads or track complex deals (custom systems, future purchases); growth constrained by lack of process
pains.verbatim: we've never really had a sales team before... our next phase of growth is like how do I action upon these leads and contacts

pains.pain: Complex deals and future-dated opportunities have no place to be tracked
pains.trigger: Customers request custom systems or want to purchase in 6 months; no CRM pipeline to hold these
pains.cost: Deals fall through the cracks; no pipeline visibility for longer-cycle opportunities
pains.verbatim: some of the deals we have are a little bit more complicated... like hey I want a custom system or I might want to do this in like six months. We don't have a good place to keep track of that now

pains.pain: Discount pricing for wholesalers and academics is informally managed
pains.trigger: Wholesaler and academic customers expect tiered pricing; currently just a tag line saying '10%'
pains.cost: Risk of reps applying wrong pricing; no enforcement or automation; will get worse as team grows
pains.verbatim: currently it's not managed in our ERP. We just usually have a tag line. I'm like, 10%. And then it's up to the people that kinda do it

workflow_reality.current_process: Inbound request arrives via help desk in HubSpot. Rep writes up the order in Brightpearl, sometimes attaches it to the help desk ticket, and sends the quote from Brightpearl. No structured deal pipeline. No formal lead-to-quote workflow. Company is mid-migration from Brightpearl to NetSuite with a HubSpot partner building out the sales process.
tools_used: Brightpearl (current quoting and order processing, being replaced)
NetSuite (ERP, future quoting and order processing)
workflow_reality.brittleness: Quoting happens outside HubSpot with no automatic link back to deals. Tax and bill-to/ship-to requirements blocked HubSpot native quoting adoption. No deal stage tracking. Mid-system migration from Brightpearl to NetSuite adds additional fragility. Entire process depends on rep discipline to manually connect systems.
workarounds: Quote in Brightpearl/NetSuite and manually attach to HubSpot help desk tickets
Track discounts via informal tag lines rather than automated price books
Calculate tax in Brightpearl/NetSuite because HubSpot cannot handle ship-to based tax
No pipeline for complex or future-dated deals; rely on memory or ad hoc tracking

alternatives_and_competitors.name: HubSpot CPQ (native quoting)
alternatives_and_competitors.context: Tried HubSpot Commerce/quoting and found it lacking: no bill-to/ship-to, broken tax calculation, limited template control. Called it 'not great.'

alternatives_and_competitors.name: Brightpearl
alternatives_and_competitors.context: Current quoting and order processing system being replaced by NetSuite. Works for quoting but creates disconnect with HubSpot CRM.

alternatives_and_competitors.name: NetSuite
alternatives_and_competitors.context: Incoming ERP and source of truth for products/pricing. Quoting in NetSuite is possible but UI is not rep-friendly. Company plans to use for order processing, not front-end quoting.

alternatives_and_competitors.name: SyncSmart
alternatives_and_competitors.context: Mentioned as a potential HubSpot-to-NetSuite sync partner. Discussed pushing payment links from NetSuite/VersaPay back to HubSpot.

quotes.text: you couldn't add a bill to and ship to... that's a huge thing for us
quotes.speaker: Sales Leader
quotes.context: Explaining why HubSpot native quoting was abandoned

quotes.text: we've never really had a sales team before... our next phase of growth is like how do I action upon these leads and these contacts and that's where we're developing now
quotes.speaker: Sales Leader
quotes.context: Describing company transition from inbound order-taking to structured sales

quotes.text: there's a disconnect between HubSpot and NetSuite... to have a quote in NetSuite is cool, but then I don't wanna have to make the reps always attach it or forget about it
quotes.speaker: Sales Leader
quotes.context: Describing the core visibility problem of disconnected systems

quotes.text: some of the deals we have are a little bit more complicated... like hey I want a custom system or I might want to do this in like six months. We don't have a good place to keep track of that now
quotes.speaker: Sales Leader
quotes.context: Describing untracked pipeline for complex and future-dated deals

quotes.text: a lot of people don't think about the managing of it. Like when you have the quotes in NetSuite, it's pretty easy to see who's paid, who hasn't paid
quotes.speaker: Sales Leader
quotes.context: Acknowledging that quote management and payment tracking matter as much as quote creation

quotes.text: I think it's process. Like, our process from even how we're live with Brightpearl right now, we go to NetSuite. That whole process of quoting is pretty easy... what I'm trying to figure out is like we've never really had a sales team before
quotes.speaker: Sales Leader
quotes.context: Identifying process as the biggest issue, not feature complexity

quotes.text: currently it's not managed in our ERP. We just usually have a tag line. I'm like, 10%. And then it's up to the people that kinda do it
quotes.speaker: Sales Leader
quotes.context: Describing informal discount management for wholesalers and academics

quotes.text: I was browsing at like 11:00... I'm like, I gotta figure out like a better way to do this
quotes.speaker: Sales Leader
quotes.context: Late-night searching for solutions indicates active frustration and urgency

value_language: don't wanna have to make the reps
a better way to do this

keyword_candidates.keyword: HubSpot quoting bill to ship to
keyword_candidates.source_context: Prospect's primary reason for abandoning HubSpot native quoting; described as 'a huge thing for us'
Tax calculation failure was a blocking issue that prevented HubSpot quoting adoption; company collects tax in 22 states
Core architectural need: quote in HubSpot, process orders in NetSuite, keep everything synced
Prospect needs custom quote templates with bill-to/ship-to fields, tax display, and product grouping
Prospect has wholesaler and academic discount tiers managed informally; needs automated price book assignment

keyword_candidates.keyword: HubSpot CPQ for small sales team
keyword_candidates.source_context: Company of 20 with 3 sales reps building their first structured sales process; need CPQ that fits their scale
Prospect's core problem: quotes in NetSuite disconnected from deals in HubSpot, reps forget to update stages
Prospect explicitly described wanting to 'put that all together from lead to quote and being able to manage it'
Lab equipment company with configure-to-order needs (custom systems), bulk pricing on consumables, and shipping/tax complexity
Prospect is mid-migration from Brightpearl to NetSuite and needs to figure out where quoting lives in the new stack
